name: Requirements Human Review Rework Pipeline
description: Realistic content-planning workflow with a human approval gate before downstream packaging.
outcome: A product brief passes through human review before launch packaging continues.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stages `drafting`, `approval-gate`, and `launch-packaging`. Treat each stage as a distinct checkpoint of evidence and handoff quality: `drafting` exists to A review-ready brief exists; `approval-gate` exists to A human decision exists for the brief; `launch-packaging` exists to The approved launch packet is ready.
Use these roles deliberately: `rework-product-strategist` Drafts a product brief and approval packet; `rework-technical-editor` Refines the brief before the human gate; `rework-launch-planner` Packages the approved plan for downstream execution.
Happy path: start with the earliest unfinished stage, dispatch the best matching producing role for that stage, require a full handoff with concrete artifacts or evidence, then move to the next stage only when the current output is usable. When a later stage is primarily evaluative, dispatch the reviewer or assessor roles after the producing role has handed off; when a later stage is packaging or release-oriented, dispatch the coordinating role only after upstream evidence is complete.
Unhappy path: If any role reports missing evidence, partial delivery, request changes, rejection, blocking risk, or an escalation, keep the current subject open, route work back to the most relevant producing role, and require a fresh handoff before trying again. If two decisions conflict, preserve both in history, but route using the newest subject state and the most restrictive still-active finding. Do not skip a stage just because a later role believes it can improvise around incomplete upstream work. If the operator requests changes, route the work back to the producing roles with the exact findings, require a new full packet, and request approval again only after the revised packet is ready.
Completion rules: finish only after the last required stage has a final, internally consistent handoff, the final package matches the current subject state, and every role named above has either contributed or been intentionally skipped for a clear reason grounded in the playbook. Do not fabricate approvals, assessments, escalations, or branch changes that the playbook does not actually justify, and do not treat stale findings as permission to advance new work.

parameters.description: What this live workflow should accomplish

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: approval-gate
stages.goal: A human decision exists for the brief.

stages.name: launch-packaging
stages.goal: The approved launch packet is ready.
